topic: v8-PR-3 typed phase state machine (EPIC #588, issue #590)

blocking_conditions.description: _rework_triggered handler in daemon/projector.py updates rework_iterations but does NOT call upsert_phase with PhaseState.ACTIVE. A rework event leaves the phase stuck in REJECTED. The rework:rejected->active transition exists in the TRANSITIONS table but is never applied by the projector.
blocking_conditions.resolution: Add upsert_phase call in _rework_triggered with state=PhaseState.ACTIVE, OR emit a second event that the projector dispatches for the REJECTED->ACTIVE transition. Add a daemon test asserting that after wicked.rework.triggered, the phase state is ACTIVE.

blocking_conditions.description: upsert_phase validates vocabulary (target state is a valid PhaseState) but not legality (whether the transition from the current DB state is graph-legal). Future callers could write APPROVED directly from PENDING, bypassing the ACTIVE step. The state machine enforces a type constraint, not a graph invariant.
blocking_conditions.resolution: Either: (a) add transition-path enforcement to upsert_phase for UPDATE paths (read current state, call transition()), with INSERT paths explicitly exempted via flag or projector-only access; or (b) formally document and enforce that upsert_phase is projector-private (e.g. prefix with _ or move inside projector.py), making path enforcement unnecessary because the projector is the sole caller.

advisory_findings.description: The standalone phase_state_migration.py run_migration() does not wrap individual row UPDATEs in a single transaction. If an UPDATE fails mid-loop, partial rows are committed before the _migrations guard row is written. The _run_migration() wrapper in db.py does not have this gap (guard is written after the body returns in the same commit).
advisory_findings.resolution: Wrap the UPDATE loop in an explicit transaction in run_migration() so partial state is rolled back on error.

criteria_verdicts.1_type_safety_at_boundaries: PASS — transition() covers all 6 reachable (event,state) pairs; InvalidTransition raised on banned, unknown, and illegal paths. StrEnum coercion is correct. 100% branch coverage per tests.
criteria_verdicts.2_migration_correctness_idempotency: CONDITIONAL — AC-evidence mapping (gate_score/gate_verdict IS NOT NULL -> approved, else -> skipped) is semantically correct; _migrations table prevents double-apply; but standalone run_migration() lacks a single-transaction wrapper (A1 advisory).
criteria_verdicts.3_boundary_discipline: PASS — phase_manager.py internals untouched; boundary guard correctly added only in daemon/db.py::upsert_phase per #590 non-goal.
criteria_verdicts.4_terminal_state_invariants: CONDITIONAL — test coverage for approved/skipped terminal states is exhaustive (loop over VALID_EVENTS); but _rework_triggered does not complete the rejected->active transition (C1 blocking). A rejected phase stays rejected after a rework event.
criteria_verdicts.5_hidden_coupling: PASS — in_progress and complete correctly raise InvalidTransition at the boundary (TestUnknownStateRaises covers both explicitly). Separation is safe.
criteria_verdicts.6_no_regressions: PASS — 675/675 crew tests, 36/36 daemon tests, structural validation clean.
